agent.downloadables_create._extract_vers.docstring: Extract version from .bumpversion.cfg in repo root.

agent.downloadables_create.model_name_fr.docstring:
Derive NIM model id (publisher/name) from a downloadable DPK name.

    DPK names are set as ``nim-{{MODEL_NAME}}-downloadable``, where MODEL_NAME is
    ``model_name.replace("/", "-")`` in create_manifest(). This reverses that.

    Example: "nim-nvidia-llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2-downloadable"
             -> "nvidia/llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2"

agent.downloadables_create._model_name_f.docstring:
Get model name from manifest path: provider folder + model folder.
    Path is like "llm/google/gemma_3_1b_it". Returns e.g. "google-gemma-3-1b-it" (_ → -).

agent.downloadables_create._alnum_suffix.docstring: Deterministic alphanumeric suffix from path (same path -> same suffix).

agent.downloadables_create._component_na.docstring:
Service, module, and panel names from the matching API model path.
    Format: s-/m-/p- + model_name (underscores→hyphens, first 28 chars) + "-" + 4 alphanumeric. ≤35 chars.
    Trailing hyphens are stripped before adding the suffix to avoid "--".

agent.downloadables_create._get_nim_entr.docstring:
Get the NIM container's entrypoint (start script) using docker inspect.
    
    Args:
        model_name: NIM model name (e.g., 'nvidia/nvclip')
    
    Returns:
        Path to the start script (e.g., '/opt/nim/start_server.sh')

agent.downloadables_create.create_manife.docstring:
Create a manifest from the template by replacing placeholders.
    
    The manifest is saved to models/downloadable/<manifest_path>/dataloop.json,
    mirroring the structure of models/api/.
    
    Args:
        model_name: NIM model name (e.g., 'nvidia/llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2')
        manifest_path: Relative path for the manifest (e.g., 'embeddings/nvidia/llama_3_2_nemoretriever_300m_embed_v2')
        image_version: Docker image version tag
        runner_image_override: If provided, use this runner image instead of generating a new one
    
    Returns:
        Manifest dictionary

agent.downloadables_create.build_docker_.signature: def build_docker_image(model_name: str, image_version: str = "1.0.0") -> str
agent.downloadables_create.build_docker_.docstring:
Build and push Docker image using docker commands.
    
    Uses docker inspect to get the NIM container's entrypoint and passes it
    as a build arg to set NIM_START_SCRIPT env var.
    
    Args:
        model_name: NIM model name (e.g. 'nvidia/nvclip' or 'baai/bge-m3').
            Slashes are replaced with hyphens for the Docker image name.
        image_version: Docker image version tag
    
    Returns:
        Target image name

agent.downloadables_create._get_api_mode.docstring: Get the name of the corresponding API model from models/api/<manifest_path>/dataloop.json.

agent.downloadables_create._get_existing.docstring:
Get the existing runner image from a manifest if it exists.
    
    Args:
        manifest_path: Relative path for the manifest (e.g., 'llm/meta/llama_3_1_8b_instruct')
    
    Returns:
        Runner image string if manifest exists, None otherwise

agent.downloadables_create.build_downloa.signature: def build_downloadable_nim(model_name: str, manifest_path: str, skip_docker: bool = False) -> dict
agent.downloadables_create.build_downloa.docstring:
Build a downloadable NIM model: Docker image and manifest.
    
    The manifest will be created at models/downloadable/<manifest_path>/dataloop.json,
    mirroring the structure of models/api/.
    
    Args:
        model_name: NIM model name (e.g., 'nvidia/llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2')
        manifest_path: Relative path for manifest (e.g., 'embeddings/nvidia/llama_3_2_nemoretriever_300m_embed_v2')
        skip_docker: If True, skip Docker build and keep existing runner image (useful for template updates)
    
    Returns:
        Manifest dictionary
    
    Example:
        # This creates:
        # - Docker image: hub.dataloop.ai/dataloop/piper/agent/runner/gpu/nvidia-llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2:<version>
        # - Manifest at: models/downloadable/embeddings/nvidia/llama_3_2_nemoretriever_300m_embed_v2/dataloop.json
        build_downloadable_nim(
            model_name="nvidia/llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2",
            manifest_path="embeddings/nvidia/llama_3_2_nemoretriever_300m_embed_v2"
        )
        
        # Update manifest from template without rebuilding Docker:
        build_downloadable_nim(
            model_name="nvidia/llama-3.2-nemoretriever-300m-embed-v2",
            manifest_path="embeddings/nvidia/llama_3_2_nemoretriever_300m_embed_v2",
            skip_docker=True
        )

agent.downloadables_create.fix_existing_.docstring:
Set service, module, and panel names in all models/downloadable/**/dataloop.json
    from app name: s-/m-/p- + app_name, each ≤35 chars. Returns number updated.

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_dpk_version.docstring:
Get the current DPK version from .bumpversion.cfg.

    Cached after first call. Strategy:
    1. GitHub API — read from **main** branch (remote, no local pull).
    2. Local .bumpversion.cfg on disk.
    3. Hardcoded fallback.

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.parse_model_id.docstring:
Parse a model ID into (publisher, model_name) with normalized casing.

    Examples:
        "nvidia/llama-3.1-70b-instruct" -> ("nvidia", "llama_3_1_70b_instruct")
        "meta/llama-3-8b"               -> ("meta", "llama_3_8b")
        "nv-embed-v1"                   -> ("nvidia", "nv_embed_v1")

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.model_to_dpk_name.docstring: Convert model ID to DPK name.  e.g. "nvidia/llama-3.1-8b" -> "nim-llama-3-1-8b".

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_model_provider.docstring:
Extract formatted provider name from model_id.

    Examples: "meta/llama" -> "Meta", "nvidia/nv-embed" -> "NVIDIA"

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_adapter_path.docstring: Get the absolute adapter file path for a model type.

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_model_folder.signature: def get_model_folder(model_id: str, model_type: str) -> str
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_model_folder.docstring:
Get the relative folder path for a model.

    Returns e.g. "models/api/llm/nvidia/llama_3_1_70b_instruct"

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_manifest_path.signature: def get_manifest_path(model_id: str, model_type: str) -> str
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.get_manifest_path.docstring: Get the relative path to dataloop.json for a model.

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.docstring:
MCP Client for generating DPK manifests for NVIDIA NIM models.

    Supports two transport modes (auto-selected by env vars):
      - HTTP: DPK_MCP_NAME is set → resolves the service URL from the Dataloop platform
      - stdio: MCP_SERVER_PATH is set → spawns a local MCP server subprocess

    Raises ValueError if neither is configured.
Resolve the MCP service URL from a deployed Dataloop DPK app.

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.signature: def _http_call_tool(self, tool_name: str, arguments: dict) -> dict
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.docstring: Call an MCP tool via HTTP JSON-RPC (platform-hosted mode).

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.signature: def _stdio_call_tool(self, tool_name: str, arguments: dict) -> dict
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.docstring: Call an MCP tool via stdio subprocess (local mode).

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.signature: def _call_tool(self, tool_name: str, arguments: dict) -> dict
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.docstring: Dispatch to HTTP or stdio based on configured mode.

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.signature: def create_nim_dpk_manifest(self, model_id: str, model_type: str, embeddings_size: int = None, license: str = None) -> dict
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.DPKGeneratorClient.docstring:
Create a DPK manifest for a NVIDIA NIM model.
        
        Args:
            model_id: NVIDIA model ID (e.g., "nvidia/llama-3.1-70b-instruct")
            model_type: Type of model ("llm", "vlm", "embedding")
            embeddings_size: Embedding dimension (only for embedding type, default 1024)
            license: Canonical Dataloop license name (e.g., "MIT", "Apache 2.0")
            
        Returns:
            dict with status, dpk_name, manifest, adapter_path, error

agent.dpk_mcp_handler.create_nim_manifes.signature: def create_nim_manifest(model_id: str, model_type: str, embeddings_size: int = None) -> dict
agent.dpk_mcp_handler.create_nim_manifes.docstring: Create NIM DPK manifest (module-level convenience function).

agent.github_client.GitHubClient.docstring:
Client for GitHub operations - creating branches, commits, and PRs.
    
    Creates PRs with proper folder structure and updates config files.

Initialize GitHub client.
        
        Args:
            token: GitHub personal access token (or from GITHUB_TOKEN env)
            repo: Repository in "owner/repo" format
            base_branch: Base branch to create PRs against

agent.github_client.GitHubClient._find_m.signature: def _find_model_folder_by_dpk_name(self, dpk_name: str, model_type: str) -> Optional[str]

Find existing model folder in repo by DPK name.
        
        Searches all dataloop.json files and matches by the 'name' field inside,
        not by folder name (since they may differ).
        
        Args:
            dpk_name: DPK name (e.g., "nv-yolox-page-elements-v1")
            model_type: Model type (used to narrow search)
            
        Returns:
            Path to dataloop.json if found, None otherwise

agent.github_client.GitHubClient._find_a.docstring:
Recursively find all dataloop.json files under a path.
        
        Returns:
            List of paths to dataloop.json files

agent.github_client.GitHubClient._update.signature: def _update_dataloop_cfg(self, existing_content: str, new_manifest_paths: List[str], deprecated_manifest_paths: List[str] = None) -> str

Update .dataloop.cfg to include new manifest paths and remove deprecated ones.

            new_manifest_paths: List of new manifest paths to add

Update .bumpversion.cfg to include new model entries and remove deprecated ones.

            new_manifest_paths: List of new manifest paths to add
            deprecated_manifest_paths: List of deprecated manifest paths to remove

Create a single PR with all new models and deprecated models.
        
        Args:
            new_models: List of dicts with model_id, model_type, manifest (passed tests)
            deprecated_models: List of dicts with model_id, model_type (deprecated models)
            failed_models: List of dicts with model_id, model_type, error (for PR body info)
            
        Returns:
            dict with pr_url, pr_number, branch_name, status, error

agent.github_client.GitHubClient._genera.signature: def _generate_unified_pr_title(self, new_models: List[Dict], deprecated_models: List[Dict]) -> str
agent.github_client.GitHubClient._genera.docstring: Generate PR title for unified PR.
Generate PR description for unified PR.

Check if a PR already exists.
        
        Args:
            model_id: Specific model to check (optional)
            branch_prefix: Branch prefix to search for
            
        Returns:
            PR URL if exists, None otherwise

agent.github_client.GitHubClient.check_m.signature: def check_model_exists(self, model_id: str, model_type: str) -> bool

Check if a model already exists in the repo.
        
        Checks both new path (models/api/) and old path (models/) for backwards compatibility.

agent.github_client.GitHubClient.close_p.docstring: Close a PR with optional comment.

agent.license_scraper._normalize_license.docstring: Normalize a raw license string to a canonical Dataloop name.

agent.license_scraper._extract_license_f.docstring: Regex-based license extraction from page text.

agent.license_scraper._fetch_modelcard_s.signature: def _fetch_modelcard_sections(model_name: str, publisher: str) -> tuple[str, str]
agent.license_scraper._fetch_modelcard_s.docstring:
Fetch build.nvidia.com modelcard page and extract license sections.

    Tries both dot and underscore URL variants since build.nvidia.com is
    inconsistent (some models use dots, others underscores in the slug).

    Returns (sections_text, url).

agent.license_scraper._llm_extract_licen.signature: def _llm_extract_license(model_name: str, page_section: str, api_key: str) -> Optional[str]
agent.license_scraper._llm_extract_licen.docstring: Ask NIM LLM to extract the license from the model card sections.

agent.license_scraper.find_license.docstring:
Find the canonical license for a model by scraping its NVIDIA model card.

    Args:
        model_name: NGC catalog model name (e.g. "deepseek-v3_1")
        publisher:  Publisher label from catalog (e.g. "DeepSeek AI")
        use_llm:    Use LLM extraction (recommended). Falls back to regex.
        api_key:    NGC API key. Defaults to NGC_API_KEY env var.

    Returns:
        Canonical license string (e.g. "NVIDIA Community Model License") or None.

agent.license_scraper.find_license_for_r.signature: def find_license_for_resource(resource: dict, use_llm: bool = True, api_key: str = None) -> Optional[str]
agent.license_scraper.find_license_for_r.docstring:
Find the license for a catalog resource dict (as returned by NGC search API).

    Args:
        resource: NGC catalog resource dict with "name" and "labels"
        use_llm:  Use LLM extraction
        api_key:  NGC API key

    Returns:
        Canonical license string or None.

agent.nim_agent._fetch_catalog_by_nim_ty.docstring: Fetch all models for a given NIM type filter (handles pagination).

agent.nim_agent.is_model_downloadable.docstring:
Check if a NIM model is in the NGC run-anywhere (downloadable) catalog.

    Accepts any form of model name:
      - DPK name:  "nim-phi-4-multimodal-instruct"
      - Model ID:  "microsoft/phi-4-multimodal-instruct"
      - NGC name:  "phi-4-multimodal-instruct"

    Returns True if the model is downloadable (run-anywhere).

agent.nim_agent.get_all_catalog_models.docstring: Get all NIM models with their availability type and license.

agent.nim_agent.get_model_ids.docstring:
Extract model IDs from model list in NVIDIA format (publisher/name).
    
    Args:
        models: List of model dicts api/downloadable_models()
    
    Returns:
        List of model IDs like "meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct"

agent.nim_agent.get_openai_nim_models.docstring:
Fetch NIM models from OpenAI-compatible API (integrate.api.nvidia.com).
    
    This is the most accurate source - returns only models with working
    OpenAI-compatible endpoints (/chat/completions, /embeddings, etc.)
    
    Args:
        api_key: NGC API key (defaults to NGC_API_KEY env var)
    
    Returns:
        List of dicts with keys: id, publisher, owned_by

agent.nim_agent.get_openai_model_ids.docstring:
Get list of model IDs from OpenAI API.
    
    Returns:
        List of model IDs like "meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct"

agent.nim_agent.get_all_repository_model.docstring:
Get all existing models from models/api (including embeddings, llm, vlm, object_detection).

    Extracts nim_model_name from configuration (embeddings, llm, vlm, object_detection).

    Returns:
        List of dicts with keys: manifest_path, nim_model_name, model_name, relative_path.
        relative_path is e.g. "embeddings/baai/bge_m3" for use under models/downloadable/.

agent.nim_agent._normalize_nim_name.docstring: Normalize NIM model name for comparison (NGC uses underscores/different punctuation).

agent.nim_agent.get_repository_downloada.docstring:
Get existing models from models/api that are run-anywhere (downloadable) in NGC.

    Returns:
        List of dicts with nim_model_name, relative_path, docker_image_name, and model_name_without_provider.
        relative_path: use for path under models/downloadable/.
        docker_image_name: nim_model_name with "/" replaced by "-" (for GCR tag).
        model_name_without_provider: last segment of nim_model_name (e.g. baai/bge-m3 -> bge-m3).

agent.nim_agent.update_support_matrix.docstring:
Update the support_matrix.md file based on current repository models.
    
    Scans all models in models/api and determines which are also available
    as downloadable (run-anywhere) by checking models/downloadable.
    
    Returns:
        Path to the updated support_matrix.md file.

agent.nim_agent.featch_report.docstring:
Fetch report for all models from OpenAI and NGC Catalog.

    Returns:
        dict: Report with the following keys:
            - openai_ids: List of OpenAI model IDs
            - api_ids: List of NGC Catalog API model IDs
            - downloadable_ids: List of NGC Catalog Downloadable model IDs
            - openai_and_downloadable: List of OpenAI and Downloadable model IDs
            - openai_and_api_only: List of OpenAI and API-only model IDs
            - openai_not_in_catalog: List of OpenAI models not in NGC Catalog
            - catalog_not_in_openai: List of NGC Catalog models not in OpenAI
            - downloadable_not_in_openai: List of Downloadable models not in OpenAI

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.docstring:
Agent that manages NVIDIA NIM model discovery and onboarding.
    
    Flow:
    1. Fetch models from NVIDIA (OpenAI API)
    2. Compare with Dataloop marketplace
    3. For each new model:
       a. Detect model type
       b. Test adapter locally
       c. Generate DPK manifest
       d. Publish and test as app
       e. Open PR if successful
    4. Report results

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.__init__.signature: def __init__(self, test_project_id: str = None, tester_auto_init: bool = True)
agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.__init__.docstring:
Args:
            test_project_id: Dataloop project ID for testing

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.fetch_models.docstring:
Fetch all supported NIM models.

        - api_models: All models from the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (source of truth
          for what is actually callable via /chat/completions, /embeddings, etc.)
        - potential_downloadable_models: (1) OpenAI ∩ NGC "run anywhere", plus (2) existing
          repo models (models/api, incl. object_detection) that are run-anywhere,
          so we compare downloadables for OD etc. with Dataloop.

        Args:
            skip_licenses: Skip per-model license scraping from NGC (faster for dry-runs).

        Populates: self.potential_api_models, self.potential_downloadable_models

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.compare.docstring:
Compare API and downloadable models with Dataloop DPKs.

        Match rule:
        - A model is "already in Dataloop" if OpenAI model id (e.g. "baai/bge-m3")
        equals a Dataloop DPK's nim_model_name.
        - Deprecated (API): Dataloop API-only DPKs whose nim_model_name is not in OpenAI ids.
        - Deprecated (Downloadable): Dataloop downloadable DPKs whose nim_model_name is not in current downloadable ids.

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.fetch_dataloop_.docstring:
Fetch all NIM DPKs from Dataloop marketplace.

        Converts dl.Dpk objects to plain dicts so the rest of the
        pipeline can use ``d["name"]`` consistently.

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent._extract_model_.docstring:
Extract core model identifier for comparison.
        
        Handles:
        - DPK names: "nim-llama3-2-90b-vision-meta" → "llama3290bvision"
        - Model IDs: "meta/llama-3.2-90b-vision-instruct" → "llama3290bvision"

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent._models_match.signature: def _models_match(self, name1: str, name2: str) -> bool
agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent._models_match.docstring: Check if two model names refer to the same model.

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.onboard_api_mod.signature: def onboard_api_model(self, model_id: str, skip_adapter_test: bool = False) -> dict
agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.onboard_api_mod.docstring:
Run full onboarding pipeline for a single model (without PR).
        
        Delegates to Tester.test_single_model which performs:
        1. Detect model type + API smoke test
        2. Test adapter locally (skipped if skip_adapter_test=True)
        3. Create DPK manifest (always when API call passes)
        4. Save manifest locally
        
        Note: Platform test (publish & test on service) is not run here for single models.
        PRs are created in batch via open_new_and_deprecated_pr() after all models are processed.
        
        Args:
            model_id: NVIDIA model ID (e.g., "nvidia/llama-3.1-70b-instruct")
            skip_adapter_test: If True, skip the adapter exec test (not thread-safe).
                The API smoke test (Step 1) is still run to validate the model.
            
        Returns:
            dict with status, model_type, dpk_name, manifest, manifest_path, steps, error

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.onboard_api_mod.signature:
def onboard_api_models(
        self, 
        models: list = None, 
        limit: int = None,
        max_workers: int = 10,
        skip_adapter_test: bool = False,
    ) -> list
agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.onboard_api_mod.docstring:
Run onboarding pipeline for multiple models in parallel.
        Uses ThreadPoolExecutor to call onboard_model() for each model concurrently.
        Note: Call open_new_and_deprecated_pr() separately after to create PRs.
        Args:
            models: List of model dicts or IDs (default: self.api_to_add from compare)
            limit: Max number of models to process
            max_workers: Max parallel workers (default: 10)
            skip_adapter_test: If False (default), run adapter tests for all models.
                Adapter tests are serialized via _ADAPTER_TEST_LOCK so they are thread-safe.
                API smoke tests (Step 1) still run in parallel across all workers.
                Set to True to skip adapter tests entirely (faster, less thorough).

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent._resolve_downlo.docstring:
Resolve relative_path for a downloadable model (e.g. 'llm/meta/llama_3_1_8b_instruct').

        If model already has 'relative_path' (repo-sourced), use it.
        Otherwise look it up from existing API manifests.

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.onboard_downloa.docstring:
Build downloadable NIM images + manifests for each model in *models*.

        For every model:
        1. Resolve the manifest_path (relative_path from models/api).
        2. Call ``build_downloadable_nim(model_name, manifest_path, skip_docker)``.
        3. Read back the created manifest and add to ``self.successful_manifests``
           so the PR step picks them up.

        Args:
            models: List of model dicts (default: self.downloadable_to_add)
            limit: Max number of models to process
            skip_docker: If True, skip Docker build (manifest-only update)

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.open_new_and_de.docstring:
Open a single unified PR for new and deprecated models.
        
        Branches from main, adds new model manifests, deletes deprecated ones,
        updates config files, and opens a PR against main.
        Returns:
            Dict with pr_result from github.create_new_and_deprecated_pr

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.run.signature:
def run(
        self,
        limit: int = None,
        open_pr: bool = True,
        max_workers: int = 10,
        skip_docker: bool = False,
    )
agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.run.docstring:
Run the complete flow.

        Args:
            limit: Max number of models to onboard (for testing)
            open_pr: Whether to open PRs after successful tests
            max_workers: Max parallel workers for testing (default: 10)
            skip_docker: If True, skip Docker build for downloadables (manifest-only)

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.run_agentic.signature:
def run_agentic(
        self,
        limit: int = None,
        open_pr: bool = True,
        max_workers: int = 10,
        skip_docker: bool = False,
        state_path: str = None,
        downloadable_preview: bool = False,
    ) -> dict
agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent.run_agentic.docstring:
State-aware variant of run().

        Same pipeline stages as run() but wrapped with:
        - State persistence (quarantine, per-model history)
        - Anomaly gate (abort if >50% of DPKs suddenly deprecated)
        - Quarantine filter (skip known-bad models, probe sample)
        - Error classification (permanent / transient / environment)
        - PR gate (skip PR when failure rate too high)
        - GitHub Actions step summary (when running in CI)

        Args:
            limit:        Max new models to onboard per run
            open_pr:      Create a PR if there are successes
            max_workers:  Parallel workers for onboarding
            skip_docker:  Skip Docker build for downloadables
            state_path:   Path to run_state.json (default: agent/run_data/run_state.json)
            downloadable_preview: Do not build downloadable manifests or docker; only print which downloadables are resolvable - For Debug usage

agent.nim_agent.NIMAgent._write_step_sum.docstring: Write a markdown summary to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY (CI only).

agent.nim_tester.Tester.docstring:
Centralized testing for NVIDIA NIM models.
    
    Tests:
    1. API call - determine type (vlm/llm/embedding/rerank)
    2. Model adapter - test with base adapters code
    3. DPK creation - via MCP
    4. if test_platform=True:  
    4.1. DPK publish to Dataloop 
    4.2. App test - test the DPK as an app
    
    Environment variables required:
    - NGC_API_KEY: NVIDIA NGC API key
    - DATALOOP_TEST_PROJECT: Dataloop project name for testing
    - OPENROUTER_API_KEY: OpenRouter API key (for MCP)

agent.nim_tester.Tester.__init__.signature: def __init__(self, api_key: str = None, dpk_mcp=None, auto_init: bool = True)
agent.nim_tester.Tester.__init__.docstring:
Args:
            api_key: NVIDIA API key
            dpk_mcp: MCP client for DPK agent
            auto_init: Automatically initialize test resources

agent.nim_tester.Tester._init_test_resou.docstring: Initialize all test resources (project, dataset, items, models) once.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_test_ite.docstring: Create test items for LLM, VLM, and embedding testing using PromptItem format.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_or_create_p.signature: def _get_or_create_prompt_item(self, dataset, folder: str, name: str, prompt_content: str) -> dl.Item
agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_or_create_p.docstring: Get existing PromptItem or create new one.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_vlm_prom.signature: def _create_vlm_prompt_item(self, image_item: dl.Item, dataset: dl.Dataset) -> dl.Item
agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_vlm_prom.docstring: Create VLM test prompt with reference to uploaded image (PromptItem format).

agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_vlm_vide.signature: def _create_vlm_video_prompt_item(self, video_item: dl.Item, dataset: dl.Dataset) -> dl.Item
agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_vlm_vide.docstring: Create VLM video test prompt with reference to uploaded video (PromptItem format).

agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_or_create_v.docstring: Get existing test video or create a simple synthetic one for VLM video testing.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_syntheti.docstring: Create a simple synthetic WebM video with colored frames for testing.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_or_create_v.docstring: Upload or get existing test image for VLM testing.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_response_fr.signature: def _get_response_from_item(self, item: dl.Item, model_name: str = None) -> str
agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_response_fr.docstring:
Get response from PromptItem.
        
        Args:
            item: The item to read response from
            model_name: Model name for PromptItem
        
        Returns:
            Response text or empty string

agent.nim_tester.Tester._find_nim_dpk.signature: def _find_nim_dpk(self, nlp: str = None) -> tuple[list[dl.Dpk], str]
agent.nim_tester.Tester._find_nim_dpk.docstring:
Find an existing NIM DPK by filtering on attributes.
        
        Filters by Category containing 'NIM' and optionally NLP attribute.
        Returns the first matching DPK name, or None if not found.
        
        Args:
            category: Category attribute to match (default: ["NIM", "Model"])
            nlp: NLP attribute to match (default: "Conversational")

agent.nim_tester.Tester._create_test_mod.docstring:
Create test model entities by cloning from existing DPKs.

        Uses _find_nim_dpk to locate DPKs by NLP attribute, with hardcoded
        fallback names if the dynamic search fails.

agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_or_create_m.signature: def _get_or_create_model(self, project: dl.Project, config: dict) -> dl.Model
agent.nim_tester.Tester._get_or_create_m.docstring:
Get existing test model or create by cloning from a DPK.
        
        Args:
            project: Dataloop project
            config: Dict with 'dpk_name' (source DPK) and 'test_model_name' (target model name)

agent.nim_tester.Tester.get_test_item_id.docstring: Get test item ID for the given model type.

agent.nim_tester.Tester.get_test_model_i.docstring: Get test model ID for the given model type.

agent.nim_tester.Tester.detect_model_typ.docstring:
Detect model type via heuristics AND verify with a real API call.

        1. Pattern-match the model name to guess the type (embedding, rerank, vlm, llm).
        2. Make a lightweight API call to confirm the model responds and gather
           extra info (e.g., embedding dimension).

        Returns:
            dict with keys:
                status:    "success" | "error" | "skipped"
                type:      "embedding" | "rerank" | "vlm" | "llm"
                response:  truncated API response (str)
                dimension: embedding dimension (only for embedding type)
                error:     error message (only on failure)

agent.nim_tester.Tester._prepare_model_e.signature: def _prepare_model_entity(self, model_type: str, nim_model_id: str, embeddings_size: int = None, nim_invoke_url: str = None)
agent.nim_tester.Tester._prepare_model_e.docstring: Prepare model entity for testing using cached resources.

agent.nim_tester.Tester.test_model_adapt.signature: def test_model_adapter(self, nim_model_id: str, model_type: str, embeddings_size: int = None, nim_invoke_url: str = None) -> dict
agent.nim_tester.Tester.test_model_adapt.docstring:
Test model adapter by executing the adapter file directly.
        
        Overrides model_id and item_id in the adapter's __main__ section with test resources,
        then executes the adapter file.
        
        Args:
            nim_model_id: NVIDIA NIM model ID
            model_type: Type of model ("llm", "vlm", "embedding")
            embeddings_size: Required for embedding models
            nim_invoke_url: Optional invoke URL for VLM models (e.g., "vlm/nvidia/neva-22b")

agent.nim_tester.Tester.publish_and_test.signature:
def publish_and_test_dpk(
        self,
        dpk_name: str,
        manifest: dict,
        model_type: str,
        cleanup: bool = True
    ) -> dict
agent.nim_tester.Tester.publish_and_test.docstring:
Publish DPK and test it as a Dataloop app.
        
        Steps:
        1. Check if DPK exists, delete if it does
        2. Publish DPK from manifest and adapter code
        3. Install DPK as an app
        4. Run prediction/embedding test
        5. Cleanup (delete app and DPK) if cleanup=True
        
        Args:
            dpk_name: Name for the DPK
            manifest: DPK manifest dict
            model_type: Type of model ("llm", "vlm", "embedding")
            cleanup: If True, delete app and DPK after testing
            
        Returns:
            dict with status, dpk_id, app_id, model_id, response, error

agent.nim_tester.Tester._cleanup_dpk_and.docstring:
Clean up DPK and app resources.
        
        Deletes in order: models -> apps -> dpk.
        If both app and dpk are passed, we clean by dpk first; the explicit app
        is usually the same one installed from this dpk, so we skip double
        uninstall and ignore 404 when touching app again.

agent.nim_tester.Tester.save_manifest_to.signature: def save_manifest_to_repo(self, model_id: str, model_type: str, manifest: dict) -> str
agent.nim_tester.Tester.save_manifest_to.docstring:
Save a manifest to the correct folder in the models/ directory.
        
        Creates the folder structure if it doesn't exist.
        Overwrites existing manifest if present.
        
        Args:
            model_id: NVIDIA model ID (e.g., "meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct")
            model_type: Model type (llm, vlm, embedding)
            manifest: DPK manifest dictionary
        
        Returns:
            Path to the saved manifest file

agent.nim_tester.Tester.post_release_pla.docstring:
Validate a random sample of public NIM DPKs on the Dataloop platform.

        Lists all public NIM DPKs, groups them by model type (llm/vlm/embedding)
        using DPK attributes, samples `percentage` of each group, installs each
        as an app using the existing public DPK (no re-publish), deploys the
        model, runs a test prediction/embedding, and reports results.

        No cleanup is performed — apps and models are left running after the test.

        Args:
            percentage: Fraction of each type group to test (default 0.10 = 10%).

        Returns:
            dict with keys "llm", "vlm", "embedding", and "summary".
            Also writes results to run_data/validate_release_{timestamp}.json.

agent.nim_tester.Tester.test_single_mode.signature:
def test_single_model(
        self,
        model_id: str,
        test_platform: bool = False,
        cleanup: bool = True,
        save_manifest: bool = True,
        skip_adapter_test: bool = False,
        license: str = None,
    )
agent.nim_tester.Tester.test_single_mode.docstring:
Test a single model: detect + API call -> adapter -> manifest -> platform.
        
        Steps:
        1. Detect model type + test API call (heuristic + smoke test in one step)
        2. Test model adapter (skipped if skip_adapter_test=True)
        3. Create DPK manifest (always when API call passed)
        4. If test_platform=True -> publish DPK and test on service (expensive)
        5. Save manifest to models/ folder (if save_manifest and API call passed)
        
        Args:
            model_id: NVIDIA model ID
            test_platform: If True, publish DPK and test on service after manifest creation. Expensive.
            cleanup: If True, cleanup DPK/app after platform test
            save_manifest: If True, save manifest JSON to models/ folder when adapter passed
            skip_adapter_test: If True, skip the adapter exec test (Step 2). Useful for
                bulk/threaded onboarding where the API smoke test (Step 1) is sufficient
                and the adapter test is not thread-safe due to shared platform resources.
        
        Environment variables required:
        - NGC_API_KEY: NVIDIA NGC API key
        - DATALOOP_TEST_PROJECT: Dataloop project name for testing
        - OPENROUTER_API_KEY: For DPK generation via MCP

agent.run_state.classify_error.docstring:
Classify an error string into a category.

    Returns:
        "permanent"  -- 404, model not found (quarantine after N failures)
        "transient"  -- timeout, rate limit, 5xx (retry next run)
        "environment" -- auth/key issues (abort entire run)

agent.run_state.RunState.docstring: Persisted state across NIM Agent runs.

agent.run_state.RunState.load.docstring: Load state from disk. No-op if file doesn't exist.

agent.run_state.RunState.save.docstring: Write state to disk (creates parent dirs if needed).

agent.run_state.RunState.record_result.signature: def record_result(self, model_id: str, status: str, error: str = None)
agent.run_state.RunState.record_result.docstring:
Record the outcome of an onboarding attempt.

        Args:
            model_id: NVIDIA model ID
            status: "success", "error", or "skipped"
            error: Error message (only for status="error")

agent.run_state.RunState.should_attempt.docstring: Return False if the model is quarantined and not due for re-probe.

agent.run_state.RunState.get_quarantined.docstring: Return list of currently quarantined model IDs.

agent.run_state.RunState.pick_probe_samp.signature: def pick_probe_sample(self, n: int = None) -> list[str]
agent.run_state.RunState.pick_probe_samp.docstring:
Pick a random sample of quarantined models to re-test.

        This lets the agent discover if quarantined models have come back online
        without re-testing the entire quarantine list every run.

agent.run_state.RunState.start_run.docstring: Mark the beginning of a new run. Returns the run record.

agent.run_state.RunState.end_run.docstring: Finalize the current run with summary data.

agent.run_state.RunState.last_run_summar.docstring: Return the most recent run record, or None.

agent.run_state.RunState.print_status.docstring: Print a human-readable summary of the current state.

agent.tests.test_agent.TestGetModelIds.docstring: Test get_model_ids which extracts publisher/name IDs.

agent.tests.test_agent.TestDetectModelTy.docstring: Verify dimension comes from actual API response length.

agent.tests.test_agent.TestNormalizeLice.docstring: Test _normalize_license maps raw strings to canonical names.

agent.tests.test_agent.TestFindLicenseFo.docstring: Test find_license_for_resource extracts publisher from labels.

models.api.base_adapter.get_downloadable.docstring:
Resolve Dataloop app route and obtain JWT-APP cookie via redirect.
    Use when the model adapter should talk to a downloadable NIM app (.apps.dataloop.ai).

    Returns:
        (base_url, cookies, session): base_url is the redirected API root;
        cookies is the session cookie jar; session is the requests.Session.

models.api.base_adapter.NIMBaseAdapter.docstring:
Base adapter for all NVIDIA NIM model types.

    Handles API/downloadable client setup and JWT session management.
    Subclasses must implement model-specific methods:
    - Embeddings: call_model_open_ai(), embed()
    - LLM/VLM: call_model(), predict(), prepare_item_func()

models.api.base_adapter.NIMBaseAdapter.g.docstring: Create OpenAI client authenticated via JWT cookie for downloadable NIM apps.

models.api.base_adapter.NIMBaseAdapter.c.docstring: Check JWT expiration and refresh session if expired or about to expire.

models.api.llm.base.ModelAdapter._flatte.signature: def _flatten_messages(self, messages: list[dict], context: str = None) -> list[dict]
models.api.llm.base.ModelAdapter._flatte.docstring:
Flatten message content from array format to plain string.
        
        Some NVIDIA NIM LLM models expect plain string content, not the 
        OpenAI multimodal format [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}].
        
        If context is provided, it will be inserted as an assistant message
        before the last user message with the format "Context: {context}".

models.api.llm.base.ModelAdapter.call_mo.signature: def call_model(self, messages: list[dict], context: str = None)
models.api.llm.base.ModelAdapter.call_mo.docstring: Call NVIDIA NIM chat completions API.

models.api.llm.base.ModelAdapter.predict.docstring: Run prediction on a batch of prompts.

models.api.vlm.base.ModelAdapter.docstring:
VLM adapter for NVIDIA NIM vision models using OpenAI-compatible API (images only).

    Supports Llama Vision, Phi Vision, and other models that use the
    standard OpenAI chat completions format with image_url in messages.
    Guided JSON output (guided_json in configuration) is sent via nvext like the LLM adapter.

models.api.vlm.base.ModelAdapter.call_mo.docstring: Call NVIDIA NIM chat completions API (images only).

models.api.vlm.base.ModelAdapter.predict.docstring: Run prediction on a batch of prompts.

models.api.vlm.base.ModelAdapter._prepar.docstring:
Normalize messages for the VLM API: keep only text and image_url content.
        Content is normalized to list format; video_url parts are skipped (images only).

models.downloadable.main._get_gpu_memory.docstring:
Get GPU memory stats using nvidia-smi.
    
    Returns:
        Tuple of (free, total, used) lists in MB, or None if nvidia-smi fails.

models.downloadable.main.Runner.docstring:
NIM runner that starts the inference server and streams logs.
    
    The server exposes an OpenAI-compatible API on port 3000.
    GPU memory is logged periodically for monitoring.

models.downloadable.tests.test_simple.ca.signature: def call_nim_endpoint(app_id: str, endpoint: str, method: str = "get", data: dict = None)
models.downloadable.tests.test_simple.ca.docstring:
Call a NIM endpoint through Dataloop's app routing.
    
    Args:
        app_id: Dataloop app ID
        endpoint: API endpoint (e.g., "/v1/embeddings", "/v1/health/live")
        method: HTTP method ("get" or "post")
        data: JSON data for POST requests
    
    Returns:
        Response object
